exercises.title: GLYPH STAMP
exercises.brief: Brand a callsign by its leading glyph.
promptLines: Define **initial(name?: string): string** returning the **first character uppercased**, or `'?'` when the name is **missing or empty**. Use `?.` and `??`.
starterLines: // TODO: also UPPERCASE the first character

tests.name: empty string -> ?
tests.code: assertEqual(initial(''), '?', 'index 0 of an empty string is undefined');

exercises.hint: name?.[0]?.toUpperCase() ?? '?'  — chain ?. so a missing name never throws.
exercises.lore: Even a one-glyph mark must burn bright. The nameless get a question.

exercises.title: NULL PROBE
promptLines: Define **safeLen(xs?: unknown[]): number** returning the array length, or `0` if the argument is missing. Use `?.` and `??`.
exercises.lore: Trust nothing that might be null.

exercises.title: FLAG SWEEP
exercises.brief: Read a deeply optional debug flag.
promptLines: Define **debugOn(cfg: { opts?: { debug?: boolean } }): boolean** returning whether debug is on, defaulting to `false` when `opts` or `debug` is missing. Use `?.` and `??`.
starterLines: function debugOn(cfg: { opts?: { debug?: boolean } }): boolean {
// TODO: the default should be false, not true
solutionLines: function debugOn(cfg: { opts?: { debug?: boolean } }): boolean {

tests.name: explicit false is kept
tests.code: assertEqual(debugOn({ opts: { debug: false } }), false, '?? keeps a real false, it does not re-default it');

exercises.hint: cfg.opts?.debug ?? false  — ?. tunnels through a missing opts in one read.
exercises.lore: Two locks deep sits the debug switch. Default it dark.

exercises.title: PRICE TAG
exercises.brief: Default a price only when it is truly absent.
promptLines: Define **priceOr(item: { price?: number }, d: number): number** returning the item's price, or the default `d` when `price` is **missing**. A price of **0** must be **kept**, never replaced. Use `??`.
starterLines: function priceOr(item: { price?: number }, d: number): number {
// TODO: fall back to d (not 0) when price is missing
solutionLines: function priceOr(item: { price?: number }, d: number): number {

tests.name: price of 0 is kept
tests.code: assertEqual(priceOr({ price: 0 }, 10), 0, 'zero is a real price; ?? must not replace it with d');

exercises.hint: item.price ?? d  — ?? only fires on null/undefined, so a price of 0 survives.
exercises.lore: A free item still costs zero. Mark it zero, not the fallback.

exercises.title: FIRST SIGNAL
exercises.brief: Take the first value that actually arrived.
promptLines: Define a generic **firstDefined<T>(a: T | undefined, b: T | undefined, c: T | undefined): T | undefined** returning the **first argument that is not `undefined`**, or `undefined` if all three are. Use `??`. A value of `0` or `''` counts as present.
starterLines: function firstDefined<T>(a: T | undefined, b: T | undefined, c: T | undefined): T | undefined {
// TODO: c must also be considered
solutionLines: function firstDefined<T>(a: T | undefined, b: T | undefined, c: T | undefined): T | undefined {

tests.name: a zero counts as present
tests.code: assertEqual(firstDefined(0, 9, 9), 0, '0 is not undefined, so ?? returns it');

exercises.hint: a ?? b ?? c  — ?? chains left to right, stopping at the first non-undefined.
exercises.lore: Three relays, one packet. Trust whichever fires first.
